---
title: "Untitled"
format: pdf
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
# install.packages("expm")
# install.packages("cmdstanr", repos = c("https://mc-stan.org/r-packages/", getOption("repos")))
# cmdstanr::install_cmdstan()
```

You can add options to executable code like this 

```{r}
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# OU Kalman likelihood benchmark against Stan using a Lyapunov stable-matrix parametrization.
#
# Model convention:
#   dX_t = -B (X_t - mu) dt + L dW_t,
# with stationary covariance S and diffusion Q satisfying
#   B S + S B' = Q.
# A general dense stable selection-strength matrix is parametrized as
#   B = (0.5 * Q + M) S^{-1},
# where S and Q are symmetric positive definite and M is skew-symmetric.
#
# The observation model is
#   Y_i = X_{t_i} + eps_i,   eps_i ~ N(0, sigma_obs^2 I).
#
# The Stan model marginalizes the latent states with a Kalman filter and uses
#   F_delta = exp(-delta * B),
#   C <- S + F_delta (C - S) F_delta'
# for the OU prediction covariance.
#
# Requirements:
#   install.packages("cmdstanr", repos = c("https://mc-stan.org/r-packages/", getOption("repos")))
#   cmdstanr::install_cmdstan()
#
# Typical usage:
#   Rscript ou_stan_lyapunov_benchmark.R
#
# You can edit the USER SETTINGS section below.

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(cmdstanr)
})

# -----------------------------
# USER SETTINGS
# -----------------------------
K <- 4L                 # OU dimension
T_obs <- 300L           # number of observations
Delta <- 0.05           # nominal mean interval for the irregular grid
dt_jitter <- 0.50       # dt ~ Uniform(Delta * (1-jitter), Delta * (1+jitter))
sigma_obs <- 0.10       # known observation noise standard deviation
m_scale <- 0.35         # magnitude of skew-symmetric non-reversible component
n_warmup <- 20L         # repeated evaluations before timing
n_timed <- 100L         # repeated evaluations for timing
seed <- 1L
compile_stan <- TRUE
run_short_sampling_check <- FALSE  # optional: verifies that the model can sample
include_priors_for_sampling <- 1L  # used only in optional sampling check

set.seed(seed)

# -----------------------------
# Small R helpers
# -----------------------------
make_spd <- function(K, diag_shift = 1.0, scale = 0.25) {
  Z <- matrix(rnorm(K * K, sd = scale), K, K)
  crossprod(Z) + diag(diag_shift, K)
}

make_skew <- function(K, scale = 0.2) {
  M <- matrix(0, K, K)
  if (K >= 2) {
    vals <- rnorm(K * (K - 1) / 2, sd = scale)
    pos <- 1L
    for (i in seq_len(K - 1L)) {
      for (j in (i + 1L):K) {
        M[i, j] <- vals[pos]
        M[j, i] <- -vals[pos]
        pos <- pos + 1L
      }
    }
  }
  M
}

make_irregular_times <- function(T_obs, Delta, jitter = 0.5) {
  if (T_obs <= 1L) {
    return(0)
  }
  dt_min <- Delta * (1 - jitter)
  dt_max <- Delta * (1 + jitter)
  if (dt_min <= 0) {
    stop("Delta * (1 - dt_jitter) must be positive")
  }
  c(0, cumsum(runif(T_obs - 1L, min = dt_min, max = dt_max)))
}

pack_skew_upper <- function(M) {
  K <- nrow(M)
  out <- numeric(K * (K - 1) / 2)
  if (K >= 2) {
    pos <- 1L
    for (i in seq_len(K - 1L)) {
      for (j in (i + 1L):K) {
        out[pos] <- M[i, j]
        pos <- pos + 1L
      }
    }
  }
  out
}

matrix_sqrt_spd <- function(S) {
  ee <- eigen(S, symmetric = TRUE)
  ee$vectors %*% diag(sqrt(pmax(ee$values, 0)), nrow(S)) %*% t(ee$vectors)
}

simulate_ou <- function(B, S, mu, times, sigma_obs) {
  K <- length(mu)
  T_obs <- length(times)
  X <- matrix(NA_real_, T_obs, K)
  Y <- matrix(NA_real_, T_obs, K)

  X[1, ] <- as.numeric(mu + matrix_sqrt_spd(S) %*% rnorm(K))
  Y[1, ] <- X[1, ] + rnorm(K, sd = sigma_obs)

  for (i in seq_len(T_obs - 1L)) {
    dt <- times[i + 1L] - times[i]
    F <- expm::expm(-dt * B)
    Q_dt <- S - F %*% S %*% t(F)
    Q_dt <- 0.5 * (Q_dt + t(Q_dt))
    X[i + 1L, ] <- as.numeric(mu + F %*% (X[i, ] - mu) + matrix_sqrt_spd(Q_dt) %*% rnorm(K))
    Y[i + 1L, ] <- X[i + 1L, ] + rnorm(K, sd = sigma_obs)
  }

  list(X = X, Y = Y)
}

median_elapsed <- function(expr, n_warmup = 10L, n_timed = 50L) {
  force(n_warmup)
  force(n_timed)
  for (i in seq_len(n_warmup)) {
    force(expr())
  }
  gc()
  times <- numeric(n_timed)
  for (i in seq_len(n_timed)) {
    t0 <- proc.time()[["elapsed"]]
    force(expr())
    times[i] <- proc.time()[["elapsed"]] - t0
  }
  c(
    median_seconds = median(times),
    mean_seconds = mean(times),
    min_seconds = min(times),
    max_seconds = max(times)
  )
}

call_log_prob <- function(mod, upars, data, jacobian = FALSE) {
  # CmdStanR has used slightly different formal names across versions.
  # These fallbacks make the script more robust.
  out <- try(mod$log_prob(upars = upars, data = data, jacobian = jacobian), silent = TRUE)
  if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) return(out)

  out <- try(mod$log_prob(upars = upars, data = data, jacobian_adjust_transform = jacobian), silent = TRUE)
  if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) return(out)

  mod$log_prob(upars, data = data, jacobian = jacobian)
}

call_grad_log_prob <- function(mod, upars, data, jacobian = FALSE) {
  out <- try(mod$grad_log_prob(upars = upars, data = data, jacobian = jacobian), silent = TRUE)
  if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) return(out)

  out <- try(mod$grad_log_prob(upars = upars, data = data, jacobian_adjust_transform = jacobian), silent = TRUE)
  if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) return(out)

  mod$grad_log_prob(upars, data = data, jacobian = jacobian)
}

call_unconstrain <- function(mod, data, variables) {
  if (is.null(mod$unconstrain_variables) || !is.function(mod$unconstrain_variables)) {
    stop(
      "CmdStanR unconstrain_variables() is not available; cannot benchmark without true unconstrained parameters.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  errors <- character()

  out <- try(mod$unconstrain_variables(data = data, variables = variables), silent = TRUE)
  if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) return(out)
  errors <- c(errors, paste("data + variables:", conditionMessage(attr(out, "condition"))))

  out <- try(mod$unconstrain_variables(data = data, init = variables), silent = TRUE)
  if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) return(out)
  errors <- c(errors, paste("data + init:", conditionMessage(attr(out, "condition"))))

  out <- try(mod$unconstrain_variables(data, variables), silent = TRUE)
  if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) return(out)
  errors <- c(errors, paste("positional:", conditionMessage(attr(out, "condition"))))

  stop(
    paste(
      "CmdStanR unconstrain_variables() failed for all supported call signatures:",
      paste(errors, collapse = "\n"),
      sep = "\n"
    ),
    call. = FALSE
  )
}
# -----------------------------
# Dependencies
# -----------------------------
if (!requireNamespace("expm", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop("Please install the expm package first: install.packages('expm')")
}

# -----------------------------
# Generate a dense stable OU model
# -----------------------------
S_true <- make_spd(K, diag_shift = 1.0, scale = 0.30)
Q_true <- make_spd(K, diag_shift = 0.5, scale = 0.20)
M_true <- make_skew(K, scale = m_scale)
B_true <- (0.5 * Q_true + M_true) %*% solve(S_true)
mu_true <- rnorm(K, sd = 0.3)
times <- make_irregular_times(T_obs, Delta, dt_jitter)

cat("True selection-strength eigenvalues:\n")
print(eigen(B_true, only.values = TRUE)$values)
cat("Minimum real part:", min(Re(eigen(B_true, only.values = TRUE)$values)), "\n\n")
if (T_obs > 1L) {
  dt <- diff(times)
  cat("Irregular time grid dt summary:\n")
  print(summary(dt))
  cat("\n")
}

sim <- simulate_ou(B_true, S_true, mu_true, times, sigma_obs)
Y <- sim$Y

stan_data <- list(
  T = T_obs,
  K = K,
  y = lapply(seq_len(T_obs), function(i) as.numeric(Y[i, ])),
  time = as.numeric(times),
  sigma_obs = sigma_obs,
  include_priors = 0L
)

# Initial constrained values. These are deliberately set near the truth so that
# timing is not dominated by pathological covariance values.
L_S_true <- t(chol(S_true))
L_Q_true <- t(chol(Q_true))
init_list <- list(
  mu = as.numeric(mu_true),
  L_S = L_S_true,
  L_Q = L_Q_true,
  m_raw = pack_skew_upper(M_true)
)

# -----------------------------
# Stan model code
# -----------------------------
stan_code <- '
functions {
  matrix symmetrize(matrix A) {
    return 0.5 * (A + A\');
  }

  matrix build_skew(vector m_raw, int K) {
    matrix[K, K] M = rep_matrix(0, K, K);
    int pos = 1;
    if (K >= 2) {
      for (i in 1:(K - 1)) {
        for (j in (i + 1):K) {
          M[i, j] = m_raw[pos];
          M[j, i] = -m_raw[pos];
          pos += 1;
        }
      }
    }
    return M;
  }
}

data {
  int<lower=1> T;
  int<lower=1> K;
  array[T] vector[K] y;
  vector[T] time;
  real<lower=0> sigma_obs;
  int<lower=0, upper=1> include_priors;
}

transformed data {
  int n_skew = K * (K - 1) / 2;
  matrix[K, K] I_K = diag_matrix(rep_vector(1.0, K));
  matrix[K, K] R = square(sigma_obs) * I_K;
}

parameters {
  vector[K] mu;
  cholesky_factor_cov[K] L_S;
  cholesky_factor_cov[K] L_Q;
  vector[n_skew] m_raw;
}

transformed parameters {
  matrix[K, K] S;
  matrix[K, K] Q;
  matrix[K, K] M;
  matrix[K, K] B;

  S = multiply_lower_tri_self_transpose(L_S);
  Q = multiply_lower_tri_self_transpose(L_Q);
  M = build_skew(m_raw, K);

  // B = (0.5 Q + M) S^{-1}; use right solve rather than explicit inverse.
  B = mdivide_right_spd(0.5 * Q + M, S);
}

model {
  vector[K] m;
  matrix[K, K] C;

  if (include_priors == 1) {
    mu ~ normal(0, 2);
    to_vector(L_S) ~ normal(0, 1);
    to_vector(L_Q) ~ normal(0, 1);
    m_raw ~ normal(0, 1);
  }

  // Stationary initialization.
  m = mu;
  C = S;

  for (i in 1:T) {
    matrix[K, K] Omega;
    matrix[K, K] L_Omega;
    matrix[K, K] C_Omega_solve_lower;
    matrix[K, K] C_Omega_inv;
    vector[K] innovation;

    Omega = symmetrize(C + R);
    L_Omega = cholesky_decompose(Omega);
    innovation = y[i] - m;
    target += multi_normal_cholesky_lpdf(y[i] | m, L_Omega);

    // Measurement update for H = I, reusing the Cholesky factor of Omega.
    C_Omega_solve_lower = mdivide_left_tri_low(L_Omega, transpose(C));
    C_Omega_inv = mdivide_right_tri_low(transpose(C_Omega_solve_lower), L_Omega);
    m = m + C_Omega_inv * innovation;
    C = symmetrize(C - C_Omega_inv * C);

    if (i < T) {
      real dt = time[i + 1] - time[i];
      matrix[K, K] F = matrix_exp(-dt * B);

      // OU stationary covariance prediction:
      // C <- S + F (C - S) F\'.
      m = mu + F * (m - mu);
      C = symmetrize(S + F * (C - S) * F\');
    }
  }
}

generated quantities {
  matrix[K, K] B_check = B;
}
'

stan_file <- file.path(tempdir(), "ou_lyapunov_kalman_benchmark.stan")
writeLines(stan_code, stan_file)
cat("Stan file written to:", stan_file, "\n")

# -----------------------------
# Compile and benchmark
# -----------------------------
if (compile_stan) {
  mod <- cmdstan_model(stan_file, force_recompile = FALSE)

  upars <- call_unconstrain(mod, stan_data, init_list)
  cat("Number of unconstrained Stan parameters:", length(upars), "\n\n")

  lp0 <- call_log_prob(mod, upars, stan_data, jacobian = FALSE)
  gp0 <- call_grad_log_prob(mod, upars, stan_data, jacobian = FALSE)
  cat("Initial log likelihood / target:", as.numeric(lp0), "\n")
  cat("Gradient vector length:", length(gp0), "\n\n")

  cat("Benchmarking log_prob...\n")
  log_prob_stats <- median_elapsed(
    expr = function() call_log_prob(mod, upars, stan_data, jacobian = FALSE),
    n_warmup = n_warmup,
    n_timed = n_timed
  )
  print(log_prob_stats)
  cat("\n")

  cat("Benchmarking grad_log_prob...\n")
  grad_stats <- median_elapsed(
    expr = function() call_grad_log_prob(mod, upars, stan_data, jacobian = FALSE),
    n_warmup = n_warmup,
    n_timed = n_timed
  )
  print(grad_stats)
  cat("\n")

  cat("Median log_prob time, ms:", 1000 * log_prob_stats[["median_seconds"]], "\n")
  cat("Median grad_log_prob time, ms:", 1000 * grad_stats[["median_seconds"]], "\n")

  if (run_short_sampling_check) {
    cat("\nRunning a short sampling check with priors enabled...\n")
    sampling_data <- stan_data
    sampling_data$include_priors <- include_priors_for_sampling
    fit <- mod$sample(
      data = sampling_data,
      chains = 1,
      parallel_chains = 1,
      iter_warmup = 100,
      iter_sampling = 100,
      refresh = 50,
      init = list(init_list)
    )
    print(fit$summary(c("mu", "B_check")))
  }
}

# -----------------------------
# Suggested columns for your paper/table
# -----------------------------
cat("\nSuggested report columns:\n")
cat("  K, T, parametrization, median log_prob ms, median grad_log_prob ms, number of unconstrained parameters\n")
cat("  parametrization = Lyapunov general dense stable B = (0.5 Q + M) S^{-1}\n")

```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
